name: Channel Stream Merger
description: Pairs quality-variant streams into a single channel with ordered fallback. Simply list your quality tags (e.g. FHD,HD,4K) in priority order — first is primary, rest are fallbacks. Supports dry-run, and auto-run after M3U refresh. Perfect for Auto channel sync
author: Dispatcharr Plugin

fields.label: Quality Tags (highest priority first)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated quality tags to search for in stream names. Left = highest priority (primary). Right = lowest (last fallback). Case-insensitive. Use [notag] as a placeholder for streams with no quality tag — place it where you want untagged streams to rank.

fields.label: Selected Groups (comma-separated)
fields.help_text: Only process channel groups matching these names (case-insensitive). Leave blank to process all auto-sync groups.

fields.label: Auto-Run After M3U Refresh
fields.help_text: When enabled, the join action triggers automatically after every M3U refresh completes.

fields.label: Verbose Logging
fields.help_text: Log every match, candidate, merge decision, and exclusion reason for troubleshooting.

actions.label: Join Paired Streams
actions.description: Find and merge quality-variant streams into single channels with ordered fallback.

confirm.title: Join Paired Streams?
confirm.message: This will merge quality-variant streams into single channels and delete redundant channels. Use 'Dry Run' first to preview.

actions.label: Dry Run (Preview)
actions.description: Preview which streams would be merged without making any changes. Check log for details.
